definition: The government agency that manages benefits. Also called MSD.
Something you must do to keep receiving your benefit.
A penalty that may reduce your benefit payment.
When your benefit is reduced because you earned income.
A waiting time before a benefit starts.
A one-off payment you do not have to pay back.
A payment you may need to pay back later.
A regular payment from Work and Income to help with living costs.
A benefit for people looking for work or unable to work due to health.
A benefit for people who have a long-term health condition or disability.
A benefit for single parents caring for children.
Extra money from Work and Income to help pay rent or housing costs.
